title: orchis spitzelii subsp. cazorlensis
intro: Orchis spitzelii subsp. cazorlensis, also known as the Cazorla Orchid, is a rare subspecies of the Orchis spitzelii plant, belonging to the Orchidaceae family. It is an orchid species native to the Iberian Peninsula and is considered an endangered plant. The medicinal properties of Orchis spitzelii subsp. cazorlensis include its potential use as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, which may help alleviate symptoms of urinary issues and joint pain. The health benefits of this plant may include improved kidney function, reduced inflammation, and relief from arthritis symptoms, and it is often prepared as an herbal infusion or tincture.
